description_long: This town hall is a two-story structure with a steeply pitched, reddish-brown tiled roof and a prominent stone clock or bell tower on one corner. The facade features a half-timbered design with exposed wooden beams and cream-colored infill, evoking a traditional European style. Large windows with decorative framing line the ground and upper floors, and the main entrance is accentuated by a small portico and double wooden doors. A large evergreen tree stands adjacent to the building, providing a natural accent. In front of the building, a stone plaza is adorned with a tall monument or signpost bearing two blue and white banners. The overall setting suggests a civic or ceremonial function, with ample space for public gatherings.
description: A traditional European-style town hall with a half-timbered facade, steep tiled roof, and a prominent stone tower, set beside a plaza with banners and a large evergreen tree.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance, the half-timbered facade, and the adjacent monument with banners, as well as the side extension and part of the stone tower.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the side of the building, the full height of the stone tower with banners, and the large evergreen tree partially obscuring the side wall.
description_view_2: The view emphasizes the rear and side of the building, showing the tiled roof, the back entrance, and the tree, with the monument and banners visible in the foreground.
description_view_3: This perspective focuses on the front and side, with a clear view of the banners, the main entrance, and the detailed half-timbered facade.
attribute_architectural_style: Tudor Revival with traditional European influences
attribute_facade_ornamentation: Exposed wooden beams, decorative window framing, and a stone tower with a lantern or clock feature
attribute_entrance_design: Single main entryway with double wooden doors, framed by a small portico and decorative timber elements
attribute_flag_display: Two organizational or municipal banners displayed on a tall monument in front of the building
